acronyms.definition: Any adverse event associated with the use of a drug in humans, whether or not considered drug related
A response to a medicinal product which is noxious and unintended
Any untoward medical occurrence in a patient administered a medicinal product
Application for approval of a generic drug product
Gene therapy, somatic cell therapy, and tissue engineered products
Application for approval of a biological product

acronyms.fullTerm: Center for Biologics Evaluation and Research
acronyms.definition: FDA center responsible for biologics, blood, vaccines

acronyms.fullTerm: Center for Drug Evaluation and Research
acronyms.definition: FDA center responsible for drugs
Codification of US federal regulations

acronyms.fullTerm: Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use
acronyms.definition: EMA scientific committee for human medicines

acronyms.fullTerm: Council for International Organizations of Medical Sciences
acronyms.definition: International organization developing PV guidance
Annual safety report during clinical development
Clinical Safety Data Management: Definitions and Standards
Data Elements for Transmission of ICSRs
Periodic Benefit-Risk Evaluation Report (PBRER)
Post-Approval Safety Data Management
Pharmacovigilance Planning
Development Safety Update Report
FDA portal for electronic regulatory submissions
Restrictive elements within a REMS

acronyms.fullTerm: European Union Drug Regulating Authorities Pharmacovigilance
acronyms.definition: EU database for ADR reports
FDA database for post-market AE reports

acronyms.fullTerm: Food and Drug Administration Amendments Act
acronyms.definition: 2007 law expanding FDA safety authorities
2012 law on drug supply chain and innovation
EU guidelines for PV activities
Medical professional (physician, pharmacist, nurse)
Organization harmonizing pharmaceutical regulations
Report of a single adverse event case
Application to conduct clinical trials
Entity holding the marketing authorization
Standardized medical terminology for AE coding
Application for approval of a new drug
US periodic safety report
ICH-harmonized periodic safety report
Agreed-upon (not required) postmarket study
Required postmarket study or clinical trial
EMA committee for safety issues
Document describing MAH's PV system
Periodic safety report (replaced by PBRER)
EU-required individual responsible for PV
US risk management program
EU risk management document
AE resulting in death, hospitalization, disability, etc.
Serious unexpected ADR in clinical trial
US system for vaccine AE reports
UN agency for international public health
UK-MHRA
UK-MHRA
UK-MHRA
UK-MHRA
UK-MHRA
UK-MHRA
UK-MHRA
UK-MHRA
UK-MHRA
FDA supplement category allowing implementation of labeling changes upon submission (CBE-0) or 30 days after submission (CBE-30)
FDA supplement allowing immediate implementation upon submission; used for safety labeling strengthening per 21 CFR 314.70(c)(6)(iii)
FDA supplement allowing implementation 30 days after submission unless FDA objects
FDA supplement requiring approval before implementation; used for major changes to approved applications
FDA requirements for content and format of prescription drug labeling per 21 CFR 201.56/201.57
EU/UK equivalent of prescribing information; standardized format per QRD template
EMA working group that develops templates for product information including SmPC, package leaflet
Systematic surveillance of scientific literature for adverse event reports and safety signals
Adverse event that results in death, is life-threatening, requires hospitalization, results in persistent disability, or is a congenital anomaly
SAE that is both suspected to be related to investigational product AND unexpected (not in IB/RSI)
Document containing clinical and nonclinical data on investigational product relevant to human study
FDA authority to require safety labeling changes; sponsor must respond within 30 days of notification
EU risk minimisation programme to prevent embryo/fetal exposure to teratogenic medicines; Includes pregnancy testing, contraception requirements, and patient acknowledgment
FDA-required programme to manage serious risks; May include ETASU for distribution/dispensing controls
Component of REMS specifying distribution, dispensing, or use requirements (e.g., iPLEDGE for isotretinoin)
FDA REMS programme for isotretinoin requiring registration, pregnancy testing, and contraception verification
Per ICH E8(R1): Attributes of a study whose integrity is fundamental to participant protection and result reliability
Approach to proactively design quality into clinical studies rather than relying solely on end-stage review
Per ICH E9(R1): Precise description of treatment effect composed of population, variable, intercurrent event handling, and summary measure
Per ICH E9(R1): Event occurring after treatment initiation that affects interpretation (e.g., treatment discontinuation, rescue medication)
Clinical trial conducted in multiple regions/countries to support global regulatory submissions per ICH E17
Per ICH E17: Evaluation that treatment effect is similar across regions included in MRCT
Attributable, Legible, Contemporaneous, Original, Accurate - core data integrity requirements
ALCOA plus Complete, Consistent, Enduring, Available - full data integrity framework
ICH initiative to modernize E6 GCP guideline; E8(R1) was first step, E6(R3) completed in 2025
EU variation with minimal/no impact; "Do and Tell" - implement then notify within 12 months
EU Type IA variation requiring immediate notification after implementation for safety monitoring
EU variation that is neither Type IA nor Type II; "Tell, Wait, Do" - 30-day waiting period
EU variation with significant impact; Requires approval before implementation (60-90 days)
FDA application pathway for generic drugs; 21 CFR 314.94; Subject to PMR/PMC per 314.98
FDA report for deviations in manufacturing of distributed biological products; 21 CFR 600.14; 45-day timeline
Comprehensive clinical safety analysis for marketing applications per ICH M4E(R2); Includes exposure, AEs, laboratory findings, vital signs

acronyms.fullTerm: Electronic Standards for Transfer of Regulatory Information
acronyms.definition: ICH M2 umbrella framework for electronic regulatory submissions including eCTD and ICSRs
Organized surveillance of blood/plasma products from donation to transfusion; Covered by Directive 2002/98/EC and GVP P.IV
Medicinal products derived from human blood/plasma; Subject to GVP P.IV and haemovigilance requirements
Modernized GCP guideline finalized May 2025; Principles-based approach; Addresses decentralized trials; Replaces E6(R2)

acronyms.fullTerm: ICH Optimization of Safety Data Collection
acronyms.definition: Guideline for selective safety data collection in late-stage trials; Allows reduced AE collection when profile well-characterized
Pre-specified adverse events requiring enhanced monitoring regardless of causality; Defined in protocol or safety monitoring plan
21 CFR 211.192 - Production record review and investigation of discrepancies; #1 FDA 483 citation; Prohibits testing into compliance
21 CFR 211.198 - Requirements for handling product quality complaints; Critical PV interface for AE identification from complaints

acronyms.fullTerm: Data Monitoring Committee / Data Safety Monitoring Board
acronyms.definition: Independent group monitoring safety during clinical trials; Reviews unblinded data; Can recommend trial modification or termination
EMA tool for MAH signal detection using EudraVigilance data; Provides eRMRs, line listings, statistical outputs
Automated statistical output from EVDAS for signal detection; Includes PRR, ROR calculations
EU/UK mandatory document describing MAH's PV system; Format per GVP Annex II; Location at QPPV site
UK post-Brexit priority pathway for innovative medicines; Includes Innovation Passport and Target Development Profile (TDP)
ILAP component: Agreed development roadmap with MHRA, NICE, and SMC; Includes PV and HTA requirements
Approval before complete data for serious conditions with unmet need; Annual renewal required; Enhanced PV obligations
EMA scheme providing early and enhanced support for promising medicines addressing unmet medical needs
Regional specifications for ICH E2B(R3) ICSR submissions; FDA, EU, UK each have specific IGs
EU database for medicinal product data; Used for ICSR product matching and identification
ICH guideline on adaptive designs including safety-driven adaptations and DMC requirements
Trial design testing multiple interventions with shared control; May add/drop arms; Per ICH E20

acronyms.fullTerm: Proportional Reporting Ratio / Reporting Odds Ratio
acronyms.definition: Disproportionality statistics for signal detection in spontaneous reporting databases
EMA master data management programme; Four services: OMS, RMS, SMS, PMS (XEVMPD)
SPOR component for unique organisation identifiers (ORG-ID, LOC-ID)
SPOR component for controlled vocabularies and code lists
SPOR component for unique substance identifiers per ISO 11238
SPOR component for medicinal product data (XEVMPD successor)
Suite of ISO standards (11615, 11616, 11238, 11239, 11240) for global product identification
International organisation of 54 regulatory authorities harmonizing GMP inspection
FDA 21 CFR 803 requirements for reporting device-related adverse events; 30-day timeline
FDA 21 CFR 820 requirements for medical device manufacturers; Includes 820.198 complaints
Determination for combination products defining lead FDA center and applicable regulations
World's largest ADR database (30M+ reports from 140+ countries); Maintained by WHO-UMC
Web-based ICSR management system for national pharmacovigilance centres; 130+ countries
Public web portal for accessing aggregated VigiBase data (vigiaccess.org)
CIOMS WG VI report on managing safety information from clinical trials
CIOMS WG IX report on practical approaches to risk minimisation for medicinal products
CIOMS WG XI report on patient involvement in drug development and safe use
21 CFR 601.12 - Requirements for changes to approved BLAs; CBE-0/CBE-30/PAS categories; Parallel to 314.70 for drugs
21 CFR 211.180 - Record retention and Annual Product Review; Complaints retained 1 year post-expiry
21 CFR 211.160 - Laboratory testing requirements; OOS investigations may reveal safety issues
FDA's active post-market surveillance system using healthcare data; 100M+ patients; Established by FDAAA 2007
FDA's primary public safety announcement mechanism; MAHs must monitor and respond
FDA labeling requirements per 21 CFR 201.57; Replaces former pregnancy categories A/B/C/D/X
21 CFR Part 11 - FDA regulation for electronic records and signatures; Critical for PV databases
Component of REMS restricting distribution, dispensing, or use; Examples: prescriber certification, patient registry
Per 21 CFR 211.180(e) - Yearly evaluation including complaint trends; May identify safety signals
Antibodies formed against biologic products; Key immunogenicity measure; Critical for biosimilar PV

acronyms.fullTerm: European Network of Centres for Pharmacoepidemiology and Pharmacovigilance
acronyms.definition: EU equivalent to FDA Sentinel; Coordinates post-authorization safety studies
US framework for marketing OTC drugs without approved NDA; Subject to 21 CFR 310.305 AE reporting

acronyms.fullTerm: Biologics Price Competition and Innovation Act
acronyms.definition: 2009 law creating US biosimilar approval pathway (351(k)); Includes interchangeability provisions
Required under PREA; Agreement between sponsor and FDA on pediatric development; Includes safety requirements
Single REMS covering multiple products (e.g., Opioid Analgesic REMS); Single administrator; Cost sharing
Technologies that enable systems to learn from data and make predictions/decisions; Increasingly used in PV for case processing, signal detection, literature monitoring
FDA CDER meeting programme for discussing AI and emerging technologies in pharmacovigilance; Part of EDSTP
CDER programme focused on AI and emerging technologies in PV; Administers EDSTM meetings
First comprehensive AI legislation; High-risk provisions apply to healthcare/PV AI; Phased implementation 2024-2027

acronyms.fullTerm: CIOMS Working Group XIV on AI in PV
acronyms.definition: International working group developing best practices for AI in pharmacovigilance; Draft under consultation 2025
Gene-modified T-cells targeting specific antigens; Key safety: CRS, ICANS, secondary malignancies; 15-year LTFU required
Systemic inflammatory response from CAR-T and other immune therapies; Grading per ASTCT; Boxed warning required
Neurologic toxicity from CAR-T therapy; Grading per ASTCT; Boxed warning required
ATMP category containing recombinant genes for therapeutic/prophylactic/diagnostic effect; Per Reg 1394/2007
ATMP containing manipulated somatic cells; Per Reg 1394/2007
ATMP containing engineered cells/tissues to repair/regenerate/replace human tissues; Per Reg 1394/2007
EMA committee providing scientific recommendations on ATMP classification, certification, assessment
Extended safety monitoring for CGT products; FDA recommends 15 years for gene therapy; Tracks delayed AEs, secondary malignancies
Japan-specific requirement: Mandatory collection of safety/efficacy data from ALL patients receiving CGT; PMDA requirement
FDA revision of 21 CFR 820 harmonized with ISO 13485:2016; Enforcement 02 Feb 2026; Impacts combination products
Canadian federal regulatory authority for drugs, biologics, medical devices, natural health products. Part of Public Health Agency of Canada.
Health Canada's national ADR reporting program. Database accessible via MedEffect. E2B(R3) compatible electronic submission.
Health Canada portal for safety communications, advisories, and public access to Canada Vigilance data.
Australia's regulatory authority for therapeutic goods (medicines, medical devices). Part of Department of Health.
TGA's publicly searchable database of adverse event reports. Australia equivalent of FAERS/EudraVigilance public access.
Switzerland's regulatory authority. ICH member. MRA with EU. ICH guidelines automatically applicable at Step 4.
Swissmedic's electronic system for pharmacovigilance reporting. E2B(R3) compatible. Mandatory for electronic ICSR submission.
Swiss Federal Act on Medicinal Products and Medical Devices. Core pharmaceutical legislation in Switzerland.
